examples: That promises to improve the lot of humanity as a whole incalculably.
This invention played an incalculably important role in the spread of culture.
But the effect of her being on those around her was incalculably diffusive: for the growing good of the world is partly dependent on unhistoric acts;
If Suhail Khan is useful to Norquist today, he was incalculably valuable in his previous capacity.
It is the problem of the small country, Muslim or non-Muslim, with the incalculably sudden big punch.
